title: Bioprinting of human pluripotent stem cell derived corneal endothelial cells with hydrazone crosslinked hyaluronic acid bioink.
specific_materials: hydrazone-crosslinked hyaluronic acid bioink with collagen IV / laminin-type additives
fabrication_method: HA-based bioink preparation followed by hydrazone crosslinking and 3D bioprinting workflow
scaffold_architecture: 3D bioprinted hydrogel construct / endothelial cell-laden bioink system
cell_type_used: human pluripotent stem cell-derived corneal endothelial-like cells
optical_metric_details: Not a main benchmarking strength from skim; verify during detailed extraction.
mechanical_metric_details: Rheological/printability properties including viscosity, shear-thinning, printability and shape fidelity.
biological_metric_details: Live/dead staining and endothelial marker expression including ZO-1, Na+/K+-ATPase, CD166 and STEM121.
main_outcomes: Bioink supported bioprinting of hPSC-derived corneal endothelial-like cells, maintained cell viability, supported endothelial marker expression and showed suitable printability/shape fidelity.
main_limitations: Mainly in vitro; limited optical benchmarking and no animal or clinical validation identified at skim stage.
extraction_notes: High-priority endothelial bioprinting paper. Strong for material/fabrication/biology/printability benchmarking; optical performance needs careful verification in full extraction.
pilot_extraction_notes: First pilot extraction row completed from skim-level full-text assessment.
